programs.name: Basketball
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop basketball play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in basketball offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: A shallow water workout using the principles of cross training and high intensity interval training, providing a high intensity workout.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Workshop: Sensory Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Join us for various sensory activities

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Crafty Creations with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Crafty Creations with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Workshop: Sensory Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Join us for various types of sensory activities

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Painting: Acrylic
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Workshop: Sensory Discovery
programs.description: Join us for various types of sensory activities

programs.name: Sensory Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you ready to discover?Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, you will discover sensory activities through play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: Bringing the principles of high-intensity interval training to the pool, this fast-paced activity uses a variety of exercises and is delivered in both shallow and deep water.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Open Studio
programs.description: Looking for a place to work on your personal art projects? Make use of our space as a shared art studio. Meet other artists and gain access to a visual art instructor who is available for guidance. A variety of visual art mediums are welcome but not all can be accommodated. Please call our community centre to confirm permitted materials. Participants bring their own art projects and supplies.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Do you have a future scientist in the family? If so, come learn about scientific theories and concepts and how to conduct experiments. In this activity, you will also get answers about the world around you and build on basics by exploring chemical reactions, flight dynamics, cellular mechanics and the inner workings of the human body. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Science - STEM
programs.description: Get ready to    explore, experiment, and discover! In this exciting STEM program, children    will explore through hands-on activities and creative challenges. From building structures    and testing simple machines to experimenting with chemical reactions and    coding basics, participants will develop problem-solving skills while having    fun. Each session encourages curiosity, teamwork, and innovation, helping    young scientists gain confidence as they ask questions, test ideas, and bring    their imaginations to life. Perfect for curious minds eager to learn by    doing!

programs.name: Science - STEM
programs.description: Get ready to    explore, experiment, and discover! In this exciting STEM program, children    will explore through hands-on activities and creative challenges. From building structures    and testing simple machines to experimenting with chemical reactions and    coding basics, participants will develop problem-solving skills while having    fun. Each session encourages curiosity, teamwork, and innovation, helping    young scientists gain confidence as they ask questions, test ideas, and bring    their imaginations to life. Perfect for curious minds eager to learn by    doing!

programs.name: Badminton - Court
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop badminton play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in badminton - court offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead. It is free to reserve a court for up to 5 players, fees may apply at the time of check in and are per person. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in. Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment. To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Toronto Women's Softball: House League B Division
programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. Participants may register individually or as part of a team. All players must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with a brief summary of their playing experience.Softball games may take place at either of the two diamonds. Full schedules will be shared with each team prior to the start of the season.

programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. This barcode is for existing players who are part of this team. Players can sign up as part of a team or individually and must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with your playing experience.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Basketball
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop basketball play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in basketball offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: A shallow water workout using the principles of cross training and high intensity interval training, providing a high intensity workout.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Workshop: Sensory Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Join us for various sensory activities

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: Want a deep-water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength-building? With the support of belts and noodles, this activity focuses on combining movement and staying afloat in deep water. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to perform a facility swim test. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Crafty Creations with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Crafty Creations with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Workshop: Sensory Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Join us for various types of sensory activities

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Painting: Acrylic
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Workshop: Sensory Discovery
programs.description: Join us for various types of sensory activities

programs.name: Sensory Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you ready to discover?Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, you will discover sensory activities through play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: Bringing the principles of high-intensity interval training to the pool, this fast-paced activity uses a variety of exercises and is delivered in both shallow and deep water.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Open Studio
programs.description: Looking for a place to work on your personal art projects? Make use of our space as a shared art studio. Meet other artists and gain access to a visual art instructor who is available for guidance. A variety of visual art mediums are welcome but not all can be accommodated. Please call our community centre to confirm permitted materials. Participants bring their own art projects and supplies.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Do you have a future scientist in the family? If so, come learn about scientific theories and concepts and how to conduct experiments. In this activity, you will also get answers about the world around you and build on basics by exploring chemical reactions, flight dynamics, cellular mechanics and the inner workings of the human body. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Science - STEM
programs.description: Get ready to    explore, experiment, and discover! In this exciting STEM program, children    will explore through hands-on activities and creative challenges. From building structures    and testing simple machines to experimenting with chemical reactions and    coding basics, participants will develop problem-solving skills while having    fun. Each session encourages curiosity, teamwork, and innovation, helping    young scientists gain confidence as they ask questions, test ideas, and bring    their imaginations to life. Perfect for curious minds eager to learn by    doing!

programs.name: Science - STEM
programs.description: Get ready to    explore, experiment, and discover! In this exciting STEM program, children    will explore through hands-on activities and creative challenges. From building structures    and testing simple machines to experimenting with chemical reactions and    coding basics, participants will develop problem-solving skills while having    fun. Each session encourages curiosity, teamwork, and innovation, helping    young scientists gain confidence as they ask questions, test ideas, and bring    their imaginations to life. Perfect for curious minds eager to learn by    doing!

programs.name: Badminton - Court
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop badminton play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in badminton - court offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead. It is free to reserve a court for up to 5 players, fees may apply at the time of check in and are per person. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in. Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment. To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
